label: Squarish 2BR / 2 baths, LD style -- private column (standard front / ensuite+common middle band / master rear), public column splits into living (front) + dining (mid) + kitchen (rear), all stacked front-to-rear -- the solver's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive -- door opens from master, no other way in. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
shared plumbing wall between the two baths -- keeps all wet stacks in one bay. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common touches standard's south face -- the bath block acts as the acoustic buffer between bedrooms. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
standard's door -- opens into living, its front-most public neighbor. Takes over the role standard<->great played in the gr sibling.

master's door -- opens into dining, its rear-most public neighbor (dining sits where great_room's rear portion used to reach into master's zone). Takes over the role master<->great played in the gr sibling.
common is publicly accessible via living (used by standard and guests). Takes over the role common<->great played in the gr sibling.
living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).
dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.

soft_proximities.note: secondary pull -- keeps the master-adjacent side of the wet core close to master. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

notes: Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked
front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of this bath-
cluster program. Originally a sibling of great_room-style
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_gr, which this topology proved strictly
better than (smaller true minimum -- 10.5x10.5 vs 11x11 -- and clean
0-warning solves at every tested size), so cl_gr was deleted the same
day and this became the sole topology for the program. Private column:
standard front, ensuite+common middle band, master rear.
Layout intent (canonical form -- brief.carport_side='right'):
Front: [standard            | living                              ]
Mid:   [ensuite | common    | dining                              ]
Rear:  [master              | kitchen                             ]
master's and standard's doors go to whichever public room occupies
that geometric role: standard->living (front), master->dining (dining
sits where a great room's rear portion would reach into master's
zone). common's public door goes to living, matching standard's
proximity ('standard can use it without going through master').
No lot_adjustment_profiles -- untuned, not needed at the verified
canonical sizes (true min 10.5x10.5; 0-warning solves at 11x11 and
